question: Let S be the set of all ternary strings over {a, b, c}. Consider all strings in S that contain at least one occurrence of two consecutive equal symbols (aa, bb, or cc). How many such strings of length 5 are possible?

understand.plain: Out of all 5-letter strings made from a, b, c, count the ones where the same letter appears twice in a row somewhere.

keywords.explain: a row of letters where each spot is one of 3 symbols
two letters sitting right next to each other
keywords.example: in abbca the two b's are adjacent

keywords.explain: it must happen somewhere - once is enough

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 648 250' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img' aria-label='Counting the no-repeat strings'><title>Counting the no-repeat strings</title><desc>First class='big ink'>3</text><text x='92' y='190' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>any letter</text><text x='150' y='130' text-anchor='middle' class='op mut'>x</text><rect x='166' y='80' width='84' understand.visual_alt: Five-spot bead diagram: 3 choices for the first letter and 2 for each later letter give 48 no-repeat strings, subtracted from 243.

given.aim: Count every 5-letter word built from a, b, c that has the same letter twice in a row somewhere.

terms.connects: the only 3 letters used to fill each spot
every word has exactly 5 spots to fill
every letter differs from the one before; we subtract these

given.plan: Start with all 243 words (U), drop the no-repeat ones (A^c), and what remains is the answer (A).

to_find: |A| = number of length-5 strings with at least one adjacent equal pair.

steps.title: Total strings |U|
steps.formula_raw: |U| = n1 x n2 x ... x nL
steps.note: each of the 5 spots has n = 3 choices

steps.title: No-repeat strings |A^c|
steps.formula_raw: |A^c| = n1 x n2 x ... x nL
steps.note: 1st spot 3 ways; every later spot must differ from the one before -> 2 ways

steps.title: At least one pair |A|
steps.note: subtract the unwanted no-repeat strings from the whole pool

formula_note: Remapped from subtraction-rule-inclusion-exclusion to the newly added complement-rule: this is 'total - none', not 2-set Inclusion-Exclusion.

question: How many 4-digit even numbers have all 4 digits distinct? A. 2240  B. 2296  C. 2620  D. 4536

understand.plain: Fill 4 slots: units must be even and the leading digit cannot be 0. Those rules clash over 0, so split into two cases (last digit = 0, or last digit in {2,4,6,8}).

keywords.explain: ranges 1000-9999, so the first digit cannot be 0

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 532 250' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img' aria-label='Two cases by the last digit'><title>Two cases by the last digit</title><desc>When the last digit is a non-zero even there are 8*8*7*4 ways; when it is 0 there are 9*8*7*1 ways; add them.</desc><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}.op{font-size:24px;font-weight:700;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='50' y='80' width='84' height='84' rx='12' class='bg'/><text x='92' y='66' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>thousands</text><text x='92' y='132' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>8</text><text x='92' y='190' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>!=0,!=units</text><text x='150' y='130' text-anchor='middle' class='op mut'>x</text><rect x='166' y='80' width='84' height='84' rx='12' class='bg'/><text x='208' y='66' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>hundreds</text><text x='208' y='132' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>8</text><text x='208' y='190' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>remaining</text><text x='266' y='130' text-anchor='middle' class='op mut'>x</text><rect x='282' y='80' width='84' height='84' rx='12' class='bg'/><text x='324' y='66' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>tens</text><text x='324' y='132' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>7</text><text x='324' y='190' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>remaining</text><text x='382' y='130' text-anchor='middle' class='op mut'>x</text><rect x='398' y='80' width='84' height='84' rx='12' class='bg'/><text x='440' y='66' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>units</text><text x='440' y='132' text-anchor='middle' class='big ink'>4</text><text x='440' y='190' text-anchor='middle' class='sub mut'>even !=0</text><text x='266' y='228' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>case last in {2,4,6,8}: 8*8*7*4 = 1792  +  case last=0: 9*8*7*1 = 504  =  2296</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Four slots filled with shrinking distinct choices, split into two cases by whether the last digit is 0, then added.

given.aim: Count 4-digit even numbers with all four digits different.

terms.connects: each filled with a digit, no repeats
clashes with units over digit 0 -> case split

given.plan: Split on whether the last digit is 0 (since 0 conflicts with no-leading-zero), fill the slots in each case with the Product Rule, then add the cases with the Sum Rule.

to_find: Number of 4-digit even numbers with distinct digits.

steps.title: Case A - last digit = 0
steps.formula_raw: n1 x n2 x n3 x n4
steps.note: units fixed to 0; thousands any of 1-9

steps.title: Case B - last digit in {2,4,6,8}
steps.formula_raw: n1 x n2 x n3 x n4

steps.title: Add the cases
steps.note: the two cases are mutually exclusive

question: The number of substrings (of all lengths inclusive) that can be formed from a character string of length n is: A. n  B. n^2  C. n(n-1)/2  D. n(n+1)/2

understand.plain: Same as the classic substring count: n of length 1, n-1 of length 2, ..., 1 of length n. Sum = n(n+1)/2.

keywords.explain: a connected piece of the string

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 560 208' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img' aria-label='Triangular sum of pieces'><title>Triangular sum of pieces</title><desc>For DOG the pieces total 3+2+1 = 6, matching n(n+1)/2.</desc><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}.op{font-size:24px;font-weight:700;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='30' y='30' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='55' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>DOG (n=3): length 1 -> 3</text><rect x='30' y='76' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='101' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>length 2 -> 2</text><rect x='30' y='122' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='147' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>length 3 -> 1</text><text x='280' y='192' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>3 + 2 + 1 = 6 = n(n+1)/2</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Pieces by length sum to n + (n-1) + ... + 1 = n(n+1)/2.

given.aim: Count the total substrings of a length-n string.
given.plan: Count pieces of each length and add them with the Sum Rule.

formula_note: Trap: option A (n) is the number of distinct lengths, not the number of pieces.

question: The number of arrangements of six identical balls in three identical bins is ______.

understand.plain: Both balls and bins are identical, so only the group SIZES matter. Just list the ways to break 6 into at most 3 piles.

keywords.explain: the bins have no labels, so (6,0,0) is the same wherever the 6 sits
writing 6 as an unordered sum of at most 3 positive parts (zeros allowed)

understand.visual_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 560 392' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img' aria-label='Listing the partitions'><title>Listing the partitions</title><desc>List the ways to split 6 identical balls into at most 3 identical bins; there are 7.</desc><style>.bg{fill:var(--cell-bg,#F1EFE8);stroke:var(--cell-line,#B4B2A9);stroke-width:2;}.hi{fill:var(--hi-bg,#CECBF6);stroke:var(--hi-line,#7F77DD);stroke-width:3;}.ok{fill:var(--ok,#1D9E75);}.no{fill:var(--no,#D85A30);}.ink{fill:var(--color-text-primary,#2C2C2A);}.mut{fill:var(--mut,#5F5E5A);}.big{font-size:26px;font-weight:700;}.lbl{font-size:18px;font-weight:600;}.sub{font-size:14px;}.tag{font-size:14px;font-weight:600;}.op{font-size:24px;font-weight:700;}text{font-family:system-ui,-apple-system,Segoe UI,sans-serif;}</style><rect x='30' y='30' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='55' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(6, 0, 0)</text><rect x='30' y='76' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='101' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(5, 1, 0)</text><rect x='30' y='122' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='147' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(4, 2, 0)</text><rect x='30' y='168' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='193' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(4, 1, 1)</text><rect x='30' y='214' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='239' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(3, 3, 0)</text><rect x='30' y='260' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='285' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(3, 2, 1)</text><rect x='30' y='306' width='500' height='36' rx='8' class='bg'/><text x='280' y='331' text-anchor='middle' class='lbl ink'>(2, 2, 2)</text><text x='280' y='376' text-anchor='middle' class='tag ink'>7 partitions of 6 into at most 3 parts</text></svg>
understand.visual_alt: Seven partitions of 6 into at most 3 parts - listed directly since there is no plug-in formula.

given.aim: Count ways to split 6 identical balls into 3 identical bins.

terms.connects: at most 3 parts in each partition
so we count integer partitions, not labelled distributions

given.plan: Because both sides are identical, enumerate the integer partitions of 6 into at most 3 parts and count them.

steps.title: Partition r into at most k parts
steps.formula_raw: p_k(r) = partitions of r into at most k parts (no closed form)
steps.note: identical balls + identical bins -> list partitions

formula_note: Trap: labelled answer C(6+3-1,6)=28 over-counts; identical bins make it the partition count 7.
